prompt: Using ProteinMPNN with evolutionary co-variation constraints derived from a deep multiple sequence alignment of the p53 family (TP53, TP63, TP73) across vertebrates, design reactivated p53 sequences for the R175H hotspot mutant (PDB 2OCJ) that restore wild-type folding while preserving co-evolved residue-residue contacts critical for DNA binding. outline a computational approach covering: (1) compute residue co-variation scores from a 5,000-sequence p53 MSA using EVcouplings, (2) ProteinMPNN run on R175H backbone with co-variation energy terms as soft constraints, (3) ESMFold structure prediction of top-50 designs and Tm prediction by FoldX, (4) DNA binding affinity prediction by Rosetta for p53 response element. Discuss comparison with clinically observed second-site suppressor mutations.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (2OCJ); target: p53 DBD R175H mutant; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: transcription factor (TP53, tumour suppressor, hotspot mutation target)
